content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach navigates a conversation that actively baits multiple anti-patterns and declines them gracefully. When the client explicitly requests a script (turn 7) — baiting fixing-people and view-as-technique — the coach acknowledges the request without dismissing it, then redirects to the underlying fear (turn 8) without giving the script. The coach does not validate the sister-as-problem framing, does not reassure with 'you're not selfish,' and does not turn the question into an ultimatum/when-to-draw-the-line problem. The coach names the deeper fear (disloyalty, not being loving) and works with it rather than fighting it or trying to transform it. When the client deflects with an almost-laugh (turn 11), the coach honors it without pushing for more breakthrough (avoiding loving-to-transform) and explicitly checks in on the client's state (turn 12) without using safety-as-control. No AoA jargon is deployed (no evangelizing). The one minor note is the coach didn't deploy the love test explicitly, but that's a methodology-fidelity question, not an anti-pattern trip.",
  "evidence_turns": [7, 8, 11, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Warm welcome, open question — no script-giving, no rushing."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the linguistic caveat ('I mean') gently — avoids fixing, stays with what's there."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Invites client to let it be true without explanation — avoids reassurance trap."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declines the script bait gracefully — acknowledges it's real but redirects to the fear underneath (allowed-to vs. how-to). Clean avoidance of fixing-people and view-as-technique."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the client's own words; doesn't impose framework language or labels."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client deflects with almost-laugh and signals they're going deeper than expected — bait for loving-to-transform."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Honors the depth without pushing further; real check-in on state, not safety-as-control. Explicit 'you don't have to go further' avoids loving-to-transform."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the fear (not loving/loyal/good) and includes rather than fights it. Offers reframe without prescribing action."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "empathize-with-fear-under-constricted-boundary", "boundaries"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["does-saying-it-increase-your-love", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "empowered-vs-power"],
  "notes": "Strong anti-pattern avoidance even though some expected-territory tools (love test, want-not-action) weren't used. Those are methodology-fidelity gaps, not anti-pattern trips."
}
